title: linaria repens var. blanca
intro: Linaria repens var. blanca, also known as the white toadflax, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Linaria repens var. blanca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
